title: Thawing of cryopreserved hPSC
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"eflhp","text":"Thawing procedure is described for one cryovial. The protocol description is general and applies to multiple hPSC lines frozen as clumps or single cells. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ee2b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7r1i1","text":"It is recommended to thaw hPSC in same culture conditions that were used before freezing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Remove cryovial containing frozen hPSC from liquid N 2 .
Sanitize the tube with 70 % volume ethanol.
Using 2 mL pipette transfer slowly hPSC suspension from the cryovial to a 15 mL conical tube.
Dropwise add 10 mL of culture media supplemented with survival factors to hPSC suspension while gently swirling the tube, refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes.
Centrifuge hPSC suspension at 300 x g, , 5 minutes
Using a 5 mL pipette gently add 2 mL of hPSC culture medium supplemented with survival factors to the pellet.
Seed hPSC at desired density according to well size/surface.
Rock the plate side to side, back and forth to spread the cells across the well.
Incubate the vessels at 37 °C and at 5 % volume CO 2 .

After 24:00:00 , perform full media change with respective hPSC maintenance media without survival factors. For further hPSC culture refer to protocol Maintenance of hPSC . hPSC display different morphology when cultured in medium containing survival factors. This morphology will change after media is replaced with medium without survival factors. For detailed morphology, refer to protocol Reference pictures of hPSC cultured in defined conditions

Thaw hPSC suspension at 37 °C using water or bead bath for about 00:02:00 - 00:04:00 leaving small amount of un-thawed media. If using water bath, carefully swirl vial in the water, avoid immersing the vial above the level of the cap.
Aspirate and remove the supernatant without disrupting the cell pellet.

Gently resuspend the hPSC pellet by pipetting up and down. After homogeneous re-suspension and if hPSC were frozen as single cells, cell counting and viability assessment using e.g. Trypan Blue 0.4%. solution can be performed.
Aspirate coating solution from the destination vessel.

Prepare coated tissue culture vessels according to hPSC line requierements and desired format. For coating procedure refer to protocol Coating of tissue Culture Vessels for hPSC It is recommended to thaw hPSC on the same matrix that was used before freezing.
Equilibrate prepared destination vessel at 37 °C until usage.

Prepare required volume of the reagents and media supplemented with survival factors according to Table 1. Additionally prepare 10 mL of media for Step 8 . Equilibrate the media to Room temperature or 37 °C . %s %s hPSC culture media: it is recommended to thaw hPSC in same media used before freezing. For options refer to protocol Maintenance of hPSC . For thawing media has to be supplemented with Survival factors. For options refer to protocol Survival factors for hPSC growth .
creator: Harald Stachelscheid
